data.title: iQIYI filed 880 invention patents in 2025, over half from AI innovation, totaling 993 new patents. #innovationleader
data.description: In Beijing on January 30, 2026, iQIYI, a top online entertainment platform in China, announced the publication of its 2025 annual patent report, highlighting a year of remarkable innovation. The company submitted a total of 993 new patents in the previous year, comprising 880 invention patents and 12 high-value ones.

data.title: Kalmar Corp to release January–December 2025 financial statements on Feb 13, 2026 at 9:00 am EET; live conference at 10:00 am EET. President & CEO Sami Niiranen and CFO Sakari Ahdekivi present the report. Access the report and register questions via provided links.
data.description: Kalmar Corporation announced that its financial statements review for January–December 2025 will be released on 13 February 2026 at around 9:00 a.m. EET. The report will be accessible on their website after publication. An international telephone conference for analysts, investors, and media is scheduled for the same day at 10:00 a.m. EET, conducted in English. President & CEO Sami Niiranen and CFO Sakari Ahdekivi will present the report. The presentation materials will be available on their website by the latest 10:00 a.m. EET. To participate and ask questions during the conference, registration is required via the provided link. Once registered, conference phone numbers and an ID will be provided for access.

data.title: Phemex launches spot trading initiative for digital gold and silver, reflecting market interest in tokenized metals.
data.description: In a recent development from Apia, Samoa on January 30, 2026, Phemex, a crypto exchange known for prioritizing user experience, announced the introduction of a unique spot trading program. The initiative, spanning a week, concentrates on digital precious metals, mirroring the increasing demand in the market for tokenized gold and silver. This move highlights the shift towards diversified digital portfolios and showcases the growing interest in alternative investment options within the cryptocurrency sphere.

data.title: BrightSign to showcase digital signage tech at ISE 2026 in Barcelona, with real-world demos for retail, QSR, transportation, and corporate sectors at booth #4S-150.
data.description: In San Jose, California, BrightSign, a leading provider of advanced digital media players and operating systems, will demonstrate the latest advancements in digital signage technology at ISE 2026 in Barcelona, Spain from February 3-6, 2026. The display, located at booth #4S-150, will feature interactive demonstrations showcasing the company's innovations in various sectors including retail, Quick Service Restaurants (QSR), transportation, and corporate settings. This event will bring together industry professionals to explore the cutting-edge solutions offered by BrightSign.

data.title: New Earth Resources Corp. (CSE: EATH) supports DOE initiative for Nuclear Innovation Campuses.
data.description: In Vancouver, British Columbia, on Jan. 30, 2026, New Earth Resources Corp. (CSE: EATH) acknowledged and praised the recent announcement by the U.S. Department of Energy about the issuance of a Request for Information (RFI). The RFI invites U.S. states to indicate their interest in hosting Nuclear Lifecycle Innovation Campuses. This development can be found at the Department of Energy's official site: https://www.energy.gov/articles/department-energy-seeks-hosts-nuclear-lifecycle-innovation-campuses.

data.title: Körber to acquire majority stake in Stellium, a Houston-based SAP supply chain management firm, to expand consulting capabilities.
data.description: In Hamburg, Germany and Houston, on Jan. 30, 2026, the global technology company Körber has officially confirmed the acquisition of a majority share in Stellium, a prominent SAP supply chain management consulting company based in Houston, Texas. This acquisition marks a significant step for Körber as it looks to expand its presence and expertise in the supply chain industry.

data.title: Stock futures down, Verizon expected to report $1.05/share on $36.06 billion revenue. SanDisk posts positive Q2 results.
data.description: Stock futures in the U.S. are down this morning, drawing attention to potential movers such as Verizon Communications Inc. (NYSE: VZ). Analysts anticipate Verizon to announce earnings of $1.05 per share on $36.06 billion in revenue before the market opens. During after-hours trading, Verizon's shares increased by 0.2% to $39.87. SanDisk Corp. (NASDAQ: SNDK) recently reported strong second-quarter results and provided a third-quarter revenue outlook of $4.4 billion to $4.8 billion, along with projected non-GAAP diluted earnings per share of $12 to $14. For more details, the complete article can be found on the Benzinga website.
